CONTENT.Honours: Borussia DortmundDFB-Pokal: 2020–21DFL-Supercup: 2019IndividualUEFA European Under-17 Championship Team of the Tournament: 2016
CONTENT.Early life: Zagadou was born in Créteil, in the southeastern suburbs of Paris, to Ivorian parents. He acquired French nationality on 8 July 2008, through the collective effect of his mother's naturalization.
CONTENT.Club career: Zagadou began his career playing at junior level for hometown club US Créteil, before signing for Paris Saint-Germain at the age of 12 in 2011. He then spent the next five seasons developing in PSG's academy, before being assigned to the reserve squad in 2016, where he made 9 appearances in the CFA.On 5 June 2017, German club Borussia Dortmund announced the signing of Zagadou on a free transfer and a five-year contract. On 28 October 2017, he scored his first goal for Dortmund in a 4–2 away loss against Hannover 96, where he also received his first red card in the 59th minute.On 12 May 2022, it was announced that Zagadou's contract would not be renewed and that he was to become a free agent.On 19 September 2022, Zagadou signed a four-year contract with VfB Stuttgart.
CONTENT.External links: Dan-Axel Zagadou at the French Football Federation (in French)Dan-Axel Zagadou at the French Football Federation (archived) (in French)Dan-Axel Zagadou on Borussia Dortmund's official websiteDan-Axel Zagadou at WorldFootball.netDan-Axel Zagadou at Soccerway
CONTENT.International career: Zagadou has represented France at U16, U17, U18, U19, U20 and U21 levels.

SUMMARY: Dan-Axel Zagadou (born 3 June 1999) is a French professional footballer who plays as a centre-back or left-back for Bundesliga club VfB Stuttgart. He has represented France from under-16 to under-21 levels.
